data preprocessing & feature engineering.question: What is data preprocessing, and why is it important before training a machine learning model?
What is feature engineering?
What are missing values, and why can they be problematic?
What is feature scaling, and why is it important?
What is normalization?
What is standardization?
What are categorical features, and how can they be encoded?
What are numerical features?
What are outliers?
Why should training and testing data be preprocessed consistently?
How would you handle missing values in different types of datasets?
Compare one-hot encoding, label encoding, and target encoding.
How would you detect and handle outliers during preprocessing?
Explain the difference between normalization and standardization, and when you would choose each.
How would you identify irrelevant or redundant features in a dataset?
What is feature selection, and why is it important?
Explain feature extraction and how it differs from feature selection.
How would you preprocess data for a real-time machine learning application?
How would you prevent data leakage during preprocessing?
Describe a complete preprocessing pipeline for a structured machine learning dataset.
How would you determine whether poor model performance is caused by weak feature engineering rather than the learning algorithm?
How would you design a scalable preprocessing pipeline for terabytes of structured data?
Explain the trade-offs between manual feature engineering and automated feature generation.
How would you evaluate whether adding new features actually improves model generalization?
How would you engineer features for time-series data while avoiding future information leakage?
Describe techniques for preprocessing datasets with thousands of features and millions of samples.
How would you design preprocessing pipelines that remain reproducible across training and production environments?
How would you detect and adapt to changes in feature distributions after deployment?
Explain how preprocessing decisions influence bias, fairness, and model interpretability.
How would you build reusable feature engineering pipelines for multiple machine learning projects?
A production model suddenly becomes inaccurate because the format of incoming data has changed. Walk me through how you would investigate and resolve the issue.
A teammate wants to remove every row containing missing values from a large customer dataset. How would you evaluate whether this is the right approach?
After deploying a new preprocessing pipeline, model accuracy decreases significantly. Explain how you would identify the root cause.
A customer provides data containing hundreds of highly correlated features. Describe how you would decide which features to keep.
A stakeholder asks you to train a fraud detection model using data that contains many extreme outliers. Explain how you would approach preprocessing.
A new feature appears to improve validation accuracy dramatically. Walk me through how you would determine whether it introduces data leakage.
Your machine learning pipeline must process millions of new records every hour. Describe how you would design preprocessing to remain efficient and reliable.
A production monitoring system detects that several important feature distributions have shifted over time. Explain your investigation and response.
A model performs well during training but poorly after deployment because preprocessing differs between environments. How would you prevent this problem?

You're designing the preprocessing and feature engineering pipeline for a global recommendation system that receives billions of events per day. Explain how you would ensure scalability, reproducibility, monitoring, and maintainability.
